title: Simulator-testable: Implement and test session renaming
description:
This feature can be fully tested on iOS simulator - no physical device needed.

Implement session renaming UI:
1. Add tap handler on conversation title to show rename dialog
2. Create rename modal/alert with text input
3. Dispatch event to update session custom-name in state
4. Persist custom name to SQLite
5. Display custom name (fallback to backend-name) throughout app

Testing approach:
- Navigate to conversation via REPL
- Tap title, enter new name
- Verify name persists after navigation away and back
- Verify name shows in session list
- Take screenshots to verify UI

Parent: react-native-29r

title: Simulator-testable: Implement and test unread message tracking
description:
This feature can be fully tested on iOS simulator - no physical device needed.

Implement unread message tracking and badge display:
1. Add unread-count field to session model in db.cljs
2. Increment unread count when messages arrive for non-active sessions
3. Clear unread count when session is opened (set-active)
4. Display unread badge in SessionListView rows
5. Display aggregate unread count in DirectoryListView

Testing approach:
- Use REPL to dispatch mock messages to non-active sessions
- Verify badge appears and increments
- Navigate to session, verify badge clears
- Take screenshots to verify UI

Parent: react-native-ev5

title: Unread message tracking and badge display
description:
iOS tracks unread message counts per session and displays badges. React Native needs:
1. Add unread-count field to session model
2. Track unread counts when messages arrive while not viewing session
3. Clear unread count when session is opened
4. Display unread badge in DirectoryListView and SessionListView

Files to modify:
- frontend/src/voice_code/db.cljs - Add unread-count to session schema
- frontend/src/voice_code/events/websocket.cljs - Update unread count on message receive
- frontend/src/voice_code/views/session_list.cljs - Show unread badge
- frontend/src/voice_code/views/directory_list.cljs - Show aggregate unread count

title: Session renaming - Add UI to set custom session names
description:
iOS allows users to rename sessions via ConversationView. The React Native app has the data model (custom-name field in session schema) but lacks:
1. UI to edit session name (tap on title in conversation view)
2. Persistence of custom names to SQLite
3. Display of custom names throughout the app

Files to modify:
- frontend/src/voice_code/views/conversation.cljs - Add rename UI
- frontend/src/voice_code/events/core.cljs - Add rename event handler
- frontend/src/voice_code/persistence.cljs - Persist custom name

title: Verify voice features on physical device
description:
Voice recognition and TTS code is fully implemented in voice.cljs but needs manual testing on real iOS device (simulator doesn't support microphone). Testing checklist:
- Start voice recognition with tap-to-speak
- Verify transcription appears in draft
- Test text-to-speech playback of responses
- Verify audio session handling (silent mode, background)
- Test voice preview in Settings

Implementation files:
- frontend/src/voice_code/voice.cljs
- frontend/src/voice_code/views/conversation.cljs (voice input area)

title: Simulator-testable: Implement and test auto-speak responses
description:
This feature can be fully tested on iOS simulator - TTS works on simulator!

Implement auto-speak for Claude responses:
1. Add :auto-speak-responses setting to db.cljs defaults
2. Add toggle in Settings view under Audio Playback section
3. In websocket response handler, check setting and dispatch :voice/speak if enabled
4. Ensure TTS doesn't speak when app is in voice input mode (prevent feedback)

Testing approach:
- Enable auto-speak in Settings
- Send a prompt via text mode
- Verify TTS speaks the response automatically
- Disable auto-speak, verify silence on next response
- Test that voice input mode suppresses auto-speak

Note: Push notifications require physical device, track separately.

Parent: react-native-gzj

title: Simulator-testable: Implement and test command history view
description:
This feature can be fully tested on iOS simulator - no physical device needed.

Implement command history view:
1. Create CommandHistory screen component
2. Create CommandOutputDetail screen component  
3. Add both screens to navigation in views/core.cljs
4. Add 'History' button to CommandMenu view
5. Fetch history via get_command_history WebSocket message
6. Display list with timestamp, exit code (color-coded), duration, preview
7. Navigate to detail view on tap, show full output

Testing approach:
- Execute some commands via CommandMenu first
- Navigate to history via REPL or UI
- Verify history list populates
- Tap item, verify detail view shows full output
- Take screenshots to verify UI

Parent: react-native-9sr

title: Simulator-testable: Implement and test message truncation
description:
This feature can be fully tested on iOS simulator - no physical device needed.

Implement message truncation for large responses:
1. Add truncation logic: first 250 chars + '...[N chars truncated]...' + last 250 chars
2. Add expanded state per message
3. Add 'Show more'/'Show less' toggle button for truncated messages
4. Keep full text in state, only truncate for rendering

Testing approach:
- Use REPL to add a very long message (>1000 chars) to a session
- Verify truncation appears with character count
- Tap 'Show more', verify full text displays
- Tap 'Show less', verify truncation returns
- Take screenshots to verify UI

Parent: react-native-fxc

title: Share Extension for file uploads
description:
iOS has VoiceCodeShareExtension that allows sharing files from other apps to voice-code. React Native needs:
1. Create native Share Extension for iOS
2. Access shared Keychain for API key (app group: group.com.910labs.untethered.resources)
3. Upload files via HTTP POST to /upload endpoint with Bearer auth
4. Show upload progress and success/error state
5. Consider react-native-share-menu or similar library

This requires native iOS development in addition to React Native code.

Files to create:
- ios/ShareExtension/ - Native Share Extension target
- frontend/src/voice_code/share_extension.cljs - JS bridge if needed

title: Message truncation for large responses
description:
iOS truncates long messages to first 250 + last 250 characters for display performance. React Native shows full text which can cause lag. Implement:
1. Add truncation logic for display (first N + '...' + last N chars)
2. Add 'Show more' / 'Show less' toggle for truncated messages
3. Keep full text in state, only truncate for rendering

Files to modify:
- frontend/src/voice_code/views/conversation.cljs - Add truncation to message-bubble component

title: Auto-speak responses and notification support
description:
iOS can auto-speak Claude responses and has push notification support with Read Aloud action. React Native needs:
1. Add auto-speak-responses setting
2. Trigger TTS when response arrives (if enabled)
3. Add react-native-push-notification library
4. Configure notification categories with Read Aloud action
5. Handle notification actions

Files to modify:
- frontend/src/voice_code/db.cljs - Add auto-speak-responses setting
- frontend/src/voice_code/views/settings.cljs - Add auto-speak toggle
- frontend/src/voice_code/events/websocket.cljs - Trigger TTS on response
- frontend/src/voice_code/notifications.cljs - New file for push notifications

title: Command history view
description:
iOS has CommandHistoryView and CommandOutputDetailView for browsing past command executions. React Native needs:
1. Add CommandHistory screen to navigation
2. Fetch command history from backend (get_command_history message)
3. Display list with timestamp, exit code, duration, output preview
4. Add CommandOutputDetail screen for full output view
5. Add navigation from CommandMenu to history

Files to modify:
- frontend/src/voice_code/views/core.cljs - Add screens to navigation
- frontend/src/voice_code/views/command_history.cljs - New file
- frontend/src/voice_code/views/command_output_detail.cljs - New file
- frontend/src/voice_code/views/command_menu.cljs - Add history button

title: Android platform testing and fixes
description:
Android project exists in frontend/android/ but has not been tested. Steps:
1. Run 'make rn-android' or 'cd frontend && npm run android'
2. Verify app builds and launches on emulator
3. Test all screens: Auth, DirectoryList, SessionList, Conversation, Settings, CommandMenu, CommandExecution, Resources, Recipes
4. Test WebSocket connection and authentication
5. Test native modules: SQLite, Keychain equivalents
6. Document any platform-specific issues and fix them

Note: Voice features may need Android-specific testing after iOS verification is complete.

title: Simulator-testable: Implement and test git branch detection
description:
This feature can be tested on iOS simulator if backend provides branch info.

Implement git branch detection in session info:
1. Check if backend already sends branch info (may need backend change)
2. If not, add git_branch field to session data from backend
3. Display branch name in SessionInfoView with copy functionality
4. Show 'Not a git repo' or similar when not applicable
5. Style branch display (maybe with branch icon)

Testing approach:
- Open SessionInfoView for session with git repo working directory
- Verify branch name displays
- Tap to copy, verify clipboard
- Test session with non-git directory
- Take screenshots to verify UI

Note: May require backend enhancement to send branch info.

Parent: react-native-dah

title: Simulator-testable: Implement and test message pruning
description:
This feature can be fully tested on iOS simulator - pure state management.

Implement message pruning to limit storage:
1. Add max-messages-per-session constant (50) to db.cljs
2. Add prune-messages helper function
3. Apply pruning after adding messages in websocket handlers
4. Apply pruning when loading session history
5. Prune oldest messages first, keep newest

Testing approach:
- Use REPL to add 60+ messages to a session
- Verify only 50 messages retained in state
- Subscribe to session, verify history limited to 50
- Check SQLite directly if needed
- Verify oldest messages pruned, newest kept

Parent: react-native-dxv

title: Simulator-testable: Implement and test draft persistence
description:
This feature can be fully tested on iOS simulator - SQLite works on simulator.

Implement draft persistence to storage:
1. Add draft save function to persistence.cljs (use settings table or new drafts table)
2. Add debounced save effect (300ms) when draft changes
3. Load drafts on app startup and populate ui/drafts state
4. Clear draft from storage when message sent
5. Handle draft cleanup when session deleted

Testing approach:
- Type a draft message in conversation
- Force-quit and restart app
- Navigate back to same session
- Verify draft text restored
- Send message, verify draft cleared
- Restart app, verify draft gone
- Take screenshots to verify

Parent: react-native-jta

title: Simulator-testable: Implement and test infer session name
description:
This feature can be fully tested on iOS simulator - no physical device needed.

Implement Claude-inferred session naming:
1. Add handler for infer_name_response message type in websocket.cljs
2. Add 'Suggest Name' button to SessionInfoView
3. Send infer_name WebSocket message when button tapped
4. Show loading state while waiting for response
5. Apply suggested name to session (same as manual rename)

Testing approach:
- Open SessionInfoView for a session with some conversation history
- Tap 'Suggest Name' button
- Verify loading indicator appears
- Verify name updates when response arrives
- Verify name persists in session list
- Take screenshots to verify UI

Parent: react-native-1kn

title: Simulator-testable: Implement and test session deletion
description:
This feature can be fully tested on iOS simulator - no physical device needed.

Implement session deletion UI:
1. Add swipe-to-delete gesture on session rows in SessionListView
2. Show confirmation dialog before delete
3. Add 'Delete Session' button in SessionInfoView
4. Dispatch event to mark session as is-user-deleted in state
5. Persist deletion to SQLite
6. Filter deleted sessions from all list subscriptions

Testing approach:
- Navigate to session list via REPL
- Swipe on a session row, confirm delete
- Verify session disappears from list
- Restart app, verify session still hidden
- Test delete from SessionInfoView as well
- Take screenshots to verify UI

Parent: react-native-6g7

title: Simulator-testable: Implement and test debug logs view
description:
This feature can be fully tested on iOS simulator - no physical device needed.

Implement debug logs view:
1. Create LogManager namespace with circular buffer (max 15KB)
2. Capture console.log/warn/error output
3. Create DebugLogs screen with ScrollView of log entries
4. Add timestamp to each log entry
5. Add 'Copy Logs' button using clipboard
6. Add 'Clear Logs' button
7. Add navigation link from Settings view

Testing approach:
- Trigger various app actions that log
- Navigate to Debug Logs via Settings
- Verify logs appear with timestamps
- Tap Copy, verify clipboard contains logs
- Tap Clear, verify logs cleared
- Take screenshots to verify UI

Parent: react-native-6ra

title: Message pruning to limit storage
description:
iOS limits messages to 50 per session in CoreData to prevent unbounded storage growth. React Native needs:
1. Add max messages per session constant (50)
2. Prune oldest messages when limit exceeded
3. Apply pruning on message receive
4. Apply pruning on session history load
5. Consider making limit configurable in settings

Files to modify:
- frontend/src/voice_code/db.cljs - Add max-messages-per-session constant
- frontend/src/voice_code/events/websocket.cljs - Add pruning logic to message handlers
- frontend/src/voice_code/persistence.cljs - Prune when saving to SQLite

title: Draft persistence to storage
description:
iOS persists message drafts to UserDefaults with debouncing so they survive app restart. React Native has drafts in memory only. Implement:
1. Save drafts to AsyncStorage or SQLite settings table
2. Debounce saves (300ms like iOS)
3. Load drafts on app startup
4. Clear draft from storage when message sent
5. Handle draft cleanup on session delete

Files to modify:
- frontend/src/voice_code/persistence.cljs - Add draft save/load functions
- frontend/src/voice_code/events/core.cljs - Add debounced draft persistence
- frontend/src/voice_code/core.cljs - Load drafts on app init

title: Background audio keep-alive for TTS
description:
iOS uses a silent audio placeholder to keep the audio session alive for TTS playback when app is in background or screen is locked. React Native needs:
1. Generate or bundle silent audio file
2. Configure audio session for background playback
3. Play silent audio on 25-second timer when TTS is active
4. Stop keep-alive when TTS completes
5. Respect 'continue playback when locked' setting

Files to modify:
- frontend/src/voice_code/voice.cljs - Add keep-alive timer logic
- ios/VoiceCodeMobile/Info.plist - Add audio background mode if not present

title: Debug logs view
description:
iOS has DebugLogsView showing last 15KB of application logs for troubleshooting. React Native needs:
1. Create LogManager to capture console output
2. Store logs in circular buffer (max 15KB)
3. Add DebugLogs screen to navigation
4. Display logs with timestamps
5. Add copy logs to clipboard button
6. Add clear logs button

Files to create:
- frontend/src/voice_code/log_manager.cljs - Log capture and storage
- frontend/src/voice_code/views/debug_logs.cljs - Debug logs screen
Files to modify:
- frontend/src/voice_code/views/core.cljs - Add screen to navigation
- frontend/src/voice_code/views/settings.cljs - Add link to debug logs

title: Session deletion UI
description:
iOS allows soft-deleting sessions (marking is_user_deleted). React Native has the schema but no UI. Implement:
1. Add swipe-to-delete on session rows
2. Add delete option in SessionInfoView
3. Dispatch persistence event to mark session deleted
4. Filter deleted sessions from lists

Files to modify:
- frontend/src/voice_code/views/session_list.cljs - Add swipe actions
- frontend/src/voice_code/views/session_info.cljs - Add delete button
- frontend/src/voice_code/events/core.cljs - Add delete event handler
- frontend/src/voice_code/subs.cljs - Filter deleted sessions

title: Infer session name from Claude
description:
iOS sends infer_name request to have Claude suggest a session name based on conversation context. React Native needs:
1. Add handler for infer_name_response message type
2. Add 'Suggest Name' button to SessionInfoView
3. Send infer_name request when button tapped
4. Apply suggested name to session

Files to modify:
- frontend/src/voice_code/events/websocket.cljs - Add infer_name_response handler
- frontend/src/voice_code/views/session_info.cljs - Add suggest name button

title: Git branch detection in session info
description:
iOS shows the current git branch in SessionInfoView when session has a working directory. React Native needs:
1. Add git branch detection (likely via backend call or native module)
2. Display branch name in SessionInfoView with copy functionality
3. Handle cases where directory is not a git repo

Files to modify:
- frontend/src/voice_code/views/session_info.cljs - Add git branch display section

title: Simulator-testable: Implement and test render loop detection
description:
This feature can be fully tested on iOS simulator - pure JS monitoring.

Implement render loop detection for performance monitoring:
1. Create performance.cljs namespace
2. Add render counter HOC or hook for key components
3. Track renders per second with sliding window
4. Log warning when >50 renders/sec detected
5. Optionally display in debug logs view
6. Add to conversation view (most likely to have issues)

Testing approach:
- Add intentional infinite re-render bug temporarily
- Verify warning logged
- Remove bug, verify no warning
- Check debug logs for performance entries

Parent: react-native-s81

title: Render loop detection for performance monitoring
description:
iOS monitors for performance issues by detecting render loops (>50 renders/sec warning). React Native needs:
1. Add render counter to key components
2. Track renders per second
3. Log warning when threshold exceeded
4. Consider using React DevTools Profiler integration
5. Add performance metrics to debug logs view

This helps identify infinite re-render bugs during development.

Files to modify:
- frontend/src/voice_code/performance.cljs - New file for performance monitoring
- frontend/src/voice_code/views/conversation.cljs - Add render tracking

title: Git worktree creation for new sessions
description:
iOS allows creating git worktrees when creating new sessions, enabling branch-isolated work. React Native needs:
1. Add 'Create Git Worktree' toggle to new session form
2. Input for worktree branch name
3. Send worktree creation request to backend (or execute locally)
4. Handle success/error states
5. Set working directory to new worktree path

Files to modify:
- frontend/src/voice_code/views/session_list.cljs - Add worktree option to create session form
- frontend/src/voice_code/events/core.cljs - Handle worktree creation logic

title: Keyboard shortcuts for macOS
description:
iOS/macOS app supports keyboard shortcuts: Cmd+. to stop speaking, Cmd+Shift+M to mute voice. React Native macOS needs:
1. Add react-native-macos support first (dependency on react-native-tah)
2. Register keyboard shortcuts using keyCommands or similar
3. Cmd+. → stop TTS playback
4. Cmd+Shift+M → toggle voice mute
5. Consider additional shortcuts for navigation

This depends on macOS platform support being added first.

Files to modify:
- frontend/src/voice_code/keyboard_shortcuts.cljs - New file for shortcut handling
- frontend/src/voice_code/core.cljs - Register shortcuts on app init

title: Haptic feedback on user actions
description:
iOS uses UINotificationFeedbackGenerator for haptic feedback on copy operations and other user actions. React Native needs:
1. Add react-native-haptic-feedback library (or use Vibration API)
2. Trigger success haptic on copy to clipboard
3. Trigger haptic on send message
4. Trigger haptic on voice recording start/stop
5. Make haptics optional via setting

Files to modify:
- frontend/package.json - Add haptic feedback dependency
- frontend/src/voice_code/haptics.cljs - New file for haptic utilities
- frontend/src/voice_code/views/session_info.cljs - Add haptic on copy
- frontend/src/voice_code/views/conversation.cljs - Add haptic on send
